channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What the ‘Bhagavad Gita’ can teach us about AI
item.description: One recent rainy afternoon, I found myself in an unexpected role—philosophy teacher to a machine. I was explaining the story of the Bhagavad Gita to a leading large language model, curious to see if it could grasp the lessons at the heart of one of the world’s most profound philosophical texts.
item.dc:creator: Faisal Hoque, Fast Company

item.title: How Camb.ai is breaking language barriers with AI
item.description: Camb.ai is on a mission to disrupt the dominance of English in global media. Founded in 2022, the AI-powered platform specializes in real-time translation that retains a speaker’s emotional resonance—processing content up to 20 times faster than traditional dubbing services.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: The Battle for AI Supremacy: OpenAI Sues Elon Musk Over Unfair Competition Claims
item.description: OpenAI sues Elon Musk over unfair competition claims, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Adobe's Agentic AI Plans: Helping, Not Replacing Human Workers
item.description: Adobe is preparing for the next generation of AI agents that will help human workers, not replace them. The tech aims to free up more creative time and unlock productivity.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How does the Nintendo Switch 2 stack up against gaming handhelds like the Steam Deck and Rog Ally X? Find out in our latest podcast episode
item.description: On the latest episode of the TechRadar Podcast, we discuss the Switch 2, tarriffs, the rumored iPhone 17 Air and Microsoft's 50th birthday celebrations.
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: Google accused of harming kids by secretly grabbing data from school-provided tech products
item.description: Nearly 70% of U.S. schools use Google's products in classrooms, lawsuit says, and numerous Bay Area school districts do, too.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Waymo gets stuck in California Chick-fil-A drive-thru
item.description: Videos showed Chick-fil-A workers standing around the stalled vehicle, which had its hazard lights on.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Deepfake porn is a labor issue
item.description: Last month, First Lady Melania Trump used her first public remarks of President Trump’s second term to voice her support for the Take It Down Act, a bipartisan bill aimed at curbing deepfake “revenge porn.” She was joined by Elliston Berry, a Texas teenager who was a victim of deepfake porn.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Wohl, Fast Company

item.title: New hands-on lab makes it easy to explore cloud configurations and workload migrations
item.description: For those that attended VMware Explore in Las Vegas and Barcelona, there was a new self-paced hands-on lab released exclusively for the attendees to experience Google Cloud VMware Engine while at the events.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Increase flexibility and enable a cyber-resilient IT infrastructure
item.description: Broadcom and Google Cloud’s continued commitment to solving our customers’ most pressing challenges stems from our joint goal to enable every organization’s ability to digitally transform through data-powered innovation with the highly secure and cyber-resilient infrastructure, platform, industry so
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Achieve greater business value with VMware Cloud Foundation on Google Cloud VMware Engine
item.description: VMware Cloud Foundation on Google Cloud VMware Engine (GCVE) is now generally available, and there has never been a better time to move your VMware workloads to Google Cloud, so you can bring down your costs and benefit from a modern cloud experience.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google Cloud Next 2025: News and insights
item.description: Google Cloud Next ’25, which runs from April 9-11 in Las Vegas) is highlighting the latest advancements and future directions of Google Cloud Platform and cloud computing.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tech risks pile up as ‘treacherous’ antitrust looms over stocks
item.description: Among the many headwinds facing big tech these days, a perhaps-unexpected one continues to nag at investors: an aggressive antitrust posture coming out of Washington.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: Travelers should prepare for extra scrutiny of devices when crossing borders, especially when entering the U.S. Experts say 100% privacy might be impossible but there things you can do to make it harder for someone to see your private data.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ignoring Wall Street, Apple gets busy on Vision Pro 2.0
item.description: While politicians everywhere seem to be doing their best to make everything pretty difficult, the North Star for Apple remains what it always has been: creating products people seem to love. That’s what got the company through (and out of) tough times before.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Scientists map part of a mouse's brain that's so complex it looks like a galaxy
item.description: Thanks to a mouse watching clips from “The Matrix,” scientists have created the largest functional map of a brain to date.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Soil from the moon's far side suggests drier conditions than the side facing Earth
item.description: Soil and rocks returned from the moon's far side suggest it may be drier than the side constantly facing Earth.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DHS says it will weigh antisemitic content in immigration applications
item.description: U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS) announced Wednesday that effective immediately, it will begin reviewing applicants’ social media accounts, using antisemitic content as grounds for denying any immigration benefits.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Shopify CEO: No new hires, unless you prove AI can't do the job
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, The Hill

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 200 million social media records leaked in major X data breach
item.description: X hit with massive data breach with 200 million records leaked, including emails. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers seven tips to help protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shopify CEO: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, NewsNation

item.title: Google targets AI inferencing opportunity with Ironwood chip
item.description: Google has unveiled a new chip — Ironwood — that could help enterprises accelerate generative AI workloads, especially inferencing — the process used by a large language model (LLM) to generate responses to a user request.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google’s Agent2Agent open protocol aims to connect disparate agents
item.description: Google has taken the covers off a new open protocol — Agent2Agent (A2A) — that aims to connect agents across disparate ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Four paradoxes of software development
item.description: Civil engineers can rightfully claim that no two bridges are exactly alike. However, bridges share many known characteristics, and the materials they are built with have known characteristics. Building bridges involves many known knowns and not as many unknown unknowns as one might think.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet
item.description: Proposed TSA security requirements mark a step in the right direction for ICS/OT security. However, stricter compliance alone isn’t enough. The post For safeguarding critical infrastructure, stronger governance alone isn’t a magic bullet first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: New observations show the asteroid that won't hit Earth resembles a spinning hockey puck
item.description: The asteroid that once had a small chance of striking Earth and now might slam into the moon resembles a spinning hockey puck.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump administration disbands crypto regulations enforcement unit
item.description: The Trump administration disbanded the National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Team (NCET) according to a Monday memo issued by the Department of Justice (DOJ) outlining their efforts to remove regulatory framework for the growing digital finance sector.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Leadership, trust, and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare
item.description: From ensuring the quality of data to fostering a culture of experimentation and adaptability, leaders must actively steer AI’s integration into operations. The post Leadership, trust, and innovation: The keys to AI’s role in modern warfare first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network
